string 2: Are you ready to confront Parallelmon?
All set!
Not yet.

We cannot all go, or coordination will prove too difficult.
Choose two fighters to participate in the battle.
Why do you try to stop me?
Resistance is futile.

I can see the future.
None may triumph over fate.

You can see the future? Don't make me laugh. 
I can't think of anything more easily refutable.

And you can say none may triumph over fate, but...
we've already come out victorious against it.

That's why we are here.
Our very existence proves you wrong.
The future... can be changed!
Get ready!

I exist across countless worlds...
I am eternal...

This Akashic Backdoor is becoming unstable,
now that its master has been defeated.

If you don't hurry and get out of there, you might end up
disappearing into some far-flung corner of space-time.

We can probably return to our own worlds now.
So... this is goodbye.
I can't tell you how much we owe you.

I feel the same way.
I hope one day we can meet again.
...I apologize for all the trouble we caused you.

I believe... we were able to remember something
very important, thanks to all of you.

Yes... something that matters more than
justice or righteousness.
You have my gratitude. Farewell.
But this is...

I want you to have it.
I've got another copy, anyway... in my brain.

I doubt you'll misuse it after all that. Besides, the theory's
incomplete. You won't be able to use it right away.

Parallelmon was capable of controlling time through their own body.
It seems they were able to compress their own data.

Evidently, they were forcing themselves to De-Digivolve to
stay small enough to avoid the stress of space-time travel.

That may not be much help now...
but maybe one day it will help us reunite.

...And that's something I'll be looking forward to—
the day we all meet again!
...Thank you. I truly cannot thank you enough.
But, before I go, allow me to ask one last thing.

That man we saw in your memories, the one who said that
"some things matter more than using righteousness as a weapon"...
...What was his name, again?
Kuremi... Kodai Kuremi.

Kodai... Kuremi.
Somehow, I feel it was his words that saved us.
I will not forget them.

If one day we do see each other again... I hope I might meet
this Kuremi, so I can express my gratitude in person.
Until then... farewell.
Well, they're gone...
Looks like it's our turn now...
We owe you our thanks, too.

Next time you see the versions of us in your world,
tell them we say hi.
No, wait— If you did that, would it cause a time paradox?

...Even if it does, I'm sure the me in your world
can handle it.
Huh...? What happened to the data?!

I recommend you get some more sleep while you can,
because there's going to be hell to pay when we get back.

I'm going to have to make sure you suffer enough
that you never get any crazy ideas like that ever again.
Eek!

...A word of advice from me: don't ever do anything
to upset the Asuna in your world.

I wish we'd gotten more of a chance to talk.
Your very existence is fascinating...
I'm sure that Agent Alpha—

Whoops! Listen to me...
I almost let slip something highly unscientific.

Anyway, so long, agent of an unknown country.
Until we meet again!

That Akashic Backdoor has been cut off
from the rest of space-time.

It should no longer function as a pathway, meaning
the threat it posed to the laws of reality has passed.
But, Parallelmon can travel across space-time.

That means it's possible that a Parallelmon born in
another timeline could start causing trouble again.

Until then, you'd better rest up, because you're sure to
have your work cut out for you again at that point.
For now, though... well done.
